t_ch: 别担心，我检查过了，这双眼睛没事，蕴含的愿力也没有太大损失。我会将它好好保管起来，稍后归还桥依小姐。
t_en: Don't worry, I've checked. The eyes are fine. They haven't lost too much Wishpower. I'll keep them safe and return them to Miss Joye later.

t_ch: 也不知道你们到底做了什么，「千禧杀手」身上的部件竟然开始互相攻击，最后只剩下桥依小姐的眼睛幸存了下来。
t_en: I have no idea what you guys did, but the components of the Millennium Killer started attacking each other. In the end, only Miss Joye's eyes survived.

t_ch: 我当侦探那么久，还几乎没见过那么令人毛骨悚然的画面…虽然是「千禧杀手」咎由自取，但你下手是真黑啊，火花小姐。
t_en: In all my years as a detective, I've rarely seen anything this bone-chilling... Though the Millennium Killer had it coming, you were absolutely brutal, Miss Sparxie.

t_ch: …说真的，你们刚才到底做了什么？
t_en: ...Seriously, what exactly did you guys do just now?

t_ch: 这个嘛…只不过是被害人们自己执行了复仇而已～
t_en: Well... The victims simply took their revenge into their own hands~

t_ch: …算了，我也不是很想知道。我们还是抓紧回去把眼睛还给桥依小姐吧。
t_en: ...Never mind, I didn't really want to know in the first place. Let's hurry back and return the eyes to Miss Joye.

t_ch: 火花小姐去找朽叶小姐报告了，我们也该行动了。
t_en: Miss Sparxie went to report to Miss Kuchiba. We should get moving too.

t_ch: 直接去诊所抓人吧。
t_en: Let's go straight to the clinic and arrest the killer.

t_ch: 嗯，「千禧杀手」应该没想到我们已经查出真相了。
t_en: Hmm, the Millennium Killer probably didn't expect us to have already uncovered the truth.

t_ch: 「千禧杀手」应该挺危险的。
t_en: The Millennium Killer must be pretty dangerous.

t_ch: 我们也不是吃素的。
t_en: We're no pushovers, either.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，我们出发吧。
t_en: Time is of the essence. Let's go.

t_ch: 因巡医生，有时间和我们聊两句吗？
t_en: Dr. Izol, do you have time for a quick chat with us?

t_ch: 不死途先生和{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}？你们怎么来了？是桥依小姐的情况恶化了吗？
t_en: Mr. Ashveil and {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}? What brings you here? Has Miss Joye's condition gotten worse?

t_ch: 你就是「千禧杀手」吧？
t_en: So you're the Millennium Killer, huh?

t_ch: 恶党，束手就擒吧！
t_en: Villain, surrender now!

t_ch: 啊？什…什么意思？
t_en: Huh? Wh—What do you mean?

t_ch: 很好，你惊慌的样子并不像是演技。那能否请你向我们解释一下，为什么「千禧杀手」的所有被害人，都曾在你的诊所接受整形手术？
t_en: Good, your panic seems genuine enough. Now, would you mind explaining to us why all the victims of the Millennium Killer had plastic surgery at your clinic?

t_ch: 「千禧杀手」的被害人？两位说的话我实在是摸不着头脑…「因巡诊所」根本就没有整形外科的科室啊。
t_en: Victims of the Millennium Killer? I can't make heads or tails of what you two are saying... Izol Clinic doesn't even have a plastic surgery department.

t_ch: 「明面上」没有而已吧。
t_en: Doesn't have one... "officially."

t_ch: …不对…难道是…不…不会是她……
t_en: ...No... Could it be... No... It can't be her...

t_ch: 你看起来知道些什么？
t_en: You seem to know something?

t_ch: 也许吧…我也并不确定。
t_en: Maybe... I'm not really sure, either.

t_ch: 想说就大声点说！
t_en: If you've got something to say, speak up!

t_ch: 抱歉，我…我自己也不是很确定。
t_en: Sorry, I... I'm not really sure myself.

t_ch: …我手下有一位医生，以前是坎贝尔幻造外科的医生。<unbreak>98</unbreak>年的时候辞职来了我们诊所，说是大医院的压力太大了。
t_en: ...I have a doctor on my team who used to be a doctor at Campbell Imagenesis Surgery. In <unbreak>1998</unbreak>, she quit and joined our clinic, saying the pressure at big hospitals was too much.

t_ch: 我其实一直都知道，她在背地里替人进行地下的整形手术…但她医疗水平很高，我一直睁一只眼闭一只眼。
t_en: I've actually known all along that she was performing underground plastic surgeries behind everyone's back... But she's highly skilled in medical care, so I just turned a blind eye.

t_ch: 但她是个很温柔的人，不可能是个连环杀手啊！
t_en: Still, she's such a gentle person. There's no way she could be a serial killer!

t_ch: 是或不是，我们亲眼见过后会得出结论的。麻烦你告诉我们，这位医生叫什么？现在人在哪里？
t_en: Whether she's a serial killer or not, we'll reach a conclusion after seeing her with our own eyes. Can you tell us, what's this doctor's name? Where is she now?

t_ch: 「巴托莉•米兰达」…她的名字叫巴托莉•米兰达。现在这个时候，她一般都会在广场附近散步……
t_en: Bartoli Miranda... Her name is Bartoli Miranda. At this hour, she usually takes walks around the Plaza...

t_ch: 好的，谢谢你的配合。
t_en: Alright, thanks for cooperating.

t_ch: 走吧，{NICKNAME}，让我们去会会这位米兰达小姐。
t_en: Let's go, {NICKNAME}, time to meet this Miss Miranda.

t_ch: 因巡医生也可能在撒谎。
t_en: Izol might be lying too.

t_ch: 别担心，他没有撒谎。我看得出来。
t_en: Don't worry, he's not lying. I can tell.

t_ch: 她会在这种时候继续散步吗？
t_en: Would she still go for a walk at a time like this?

t_ch: 很多连环杀手都执着于「规律性」，即使到了这种关键时刻，她恐怕也不会突然改变自己的生活习惯吧。
t_en: Many serial killers are obsessed with "routines." Even at such a crucial moment, she probably wouldn't suddenly change her lifestyle habits.

t_ch: 请问是巴托莉•米兰达小姐吗？
t_en: Excuse me, are you Miss Bartoli Miranda?

t_ch: 看来，人还是不该有太规律的作息，不然很容易被侦探找到。
t_en: Guess having too much of a routine isn't the best idea. Makes it way too easy for detectives to track you down.

t_ch: 呵，要是没做亏心事，应该也不怕被侦探找到吧？「千禧杀手」。
t_en: Of course, if you've got nothing to hide, you shouldn't be afraid of a detective tracking you down, right? "Millennium Killer."

t_ch: 我一直很讨厌这个外号。就因为案发在<unbreak>1999</unbreak>年，所以起名叫「千禧」？就因为死了很多幻造种，就要称我为「杀手」？
t_en: I've always hated that moniker. Just because the incident happened in <unbreak>1999</unbreak>, they went with "Millennium"? Just because many imagenae died, they had to label me a "killer"?

t_ch: 呃…不然呢？
t_en: Uh... Were they supposed to call you something else?

t_ch: 我的行为中不包含任何「杀意」，也从来都没有想过要「杀死」谁，他们自己扛不住而已。
t_en: There's no malice in my actions, and I've never thought about "killing" anyone. They just couldn't handle it, that's all.

t_ch: 你都不狡辩一下的吗？
t_en: You're not even going to try to talk your way out of it?

t_ch: 不。我并不是为了和蠢货斗嘴才走到今天的。
t_en: No. I didn't get to where I am today just to bicker with fools.

t_ch: 看在你们两位也不是凡夫俗子的份上，我倒是不介意给你们讲讲我为何要这么做。
t_en: Since you two aren't just any random nobodies, I suppose I don't mind explaining why I did this.

t_ch: 我曾是一名优秀的整形医生。我的大部分患者，最终都能成为受人瞩目的明星。
t_en: I used to be an excellent plastic surgeon. Most of my patients ended up becoming prominent, head-turning stars.

t_ch: 但无论这些只能受我恩惠的无用之辈多么受人瞩目、多么光芒万丈，我都只是个籍籍无名的医生罢了。
t_en: Yet, regardless of how much attention and glamour these worthless people — who owe all their success to the favors I've so kindly bestowed upon them — receive, I am still just a nameless doctor.

t_ch: 这种生涯让我稍微心理失衡了一段时间，但我很快就自己调理好了。人不能总是内耗嘛！
t_en: This career path threw me off balance for a while, but I pulled myself together pretty quickly. You can't keep tearing yourself apart!

t_ch: 我想到，也许自己可以不去做一个「奉献者」，而是做一个「播种者」。
t_en: I thought, maybe instead of being a "benefactor," I could be a "sower."

t_ch: 于是我来到了因巡的诊所，在这里，我悄悄将自己需要的部位播种给那些渴望成名的蠢货。
t_en: So I came to Izol's clinic, where I secretly planted the parts I needed in those fame-hungry fools.

t_ch: 等到他们真的成名，那些由我创造的、最受人瞩目的部位，早已满盈着愿力，等待着我的采摘。
t_en: When they truly become famous, those eye-catching parts I created will already be brimming with Wishpower, ripe for my harvest.

t_ch: 所以都说了，我根本不是什么「杀手」，我只是在践行「春种秋收」的自然之理。等到全部采摘完成——也即是现在，我就将它们全部都移植到自己身上。
t_en: So, I've made it clear. I'm not a "killer." I'm merely following the natural law of "sowing in spring and reaping in autumn." Once the harvest is complete — that is, now — I will transplant all these parts onto myself.

t_ch: 哈哈，不觉得有一种丰收的喜悦吗？
t_en: Haha, doesn't it give you the joy that a bountiful harvest brings?

t_ch: 如今，我的全身都是二相乐园「最受人瞩目的部位」。在这个注意力为王的时代，我已登基加冕，成为「视线的君王」。
t_en: Now, my body is entirely made up of the most eye-catching parts in Planarcadia. In this age where attention is king, I have taken the throne. I crown myself, "Ruler of Gazes."

t_ch: 我不怕治安局、不怕异常防御部，更不怕你们这些所谓的谒者。我是迈向神境之人，尘世的法律无法制裁我。
t_en: I fear neither the Security Bureau nor the Department of Aberration Defense, and least of all you so-called Supplicants. I am ascending to godhood, and mortal laws cannot bind me.

t_ch: 你充其量是个神人。
t_en: You're just a god-awful lunatic at best.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，骂得好。
t_en: Hahaha, you tell 'em!

t_ch: 迈向神境哪有那么容易…
t_en: Ascending to godhood isn't that easy...

t_ch: 是啊，这家伙只是沉浸在自己的妄想中，不知天地为何物了。
t_en: Yeah, this person's just wrapped up in her own delusions, completely out of touch with reality.

t_ch: 真是不出所料的反应。无所谓，我也不打算和死人争辩。
t_en: Just the reaction I expected. Whatever, I'm not going to argue with the dead.

t_ch: 只是，别再叫我「千禧杀手」了。也别再叫我「巴托莉•米兰达」。我已经有了全新的名字，属于「视线的君王」的尊号！
t_en: Just... stop calling me the Millennium Killer. And don't call me "Bartoli Miranda" anymore, either. I have a brand-new name now, a noble title befitting the "Ruler of Gazes"!

t_ch: 「弗兰肯Official」！
t_en: "Franken_Official"!

t_ch: 好蠢的名字…
t_en: What a dumb name...

t_ch: 好丑的外形…
t_en: What an ugly appearance...

t_ch: 你杀了那么多幻造种，最后就把自己拼凑成这玩意儿？你自己觉得这好看吗？
t_en: After killing so many imagenae, this is what you cobbled yourself together into? Do you actually think this looks good?

t_ch: 尽情嘲讽吧！我会用仁慈的死亡给予你们宽恕！
t_en: Mock me all you want! I shall grant you forgiveness through a merciful death!

t_ch: 唉…跟纯疯子真是无法交流。准备应战吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: *sigh* There's just no reasoning with a complete lunatic. Get ready for battle, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不对…为什么…我维持不住自己的形态了……
t_en: Something's wrong... Why... I can't maintain my form anymore...

t_ch: 为什么我的愿力结构在崩溃？不可能，我的幻造技术是完美的……
t_en: Why is my Wishpower structure collapsing? Impossible, my imagenesis technique is perfect...

t_ch: 好…好难受…我不能…死在这里……
t_en: Ugh... I feel... terrible... I can't... die here...

t_ch: 糟糕…好像下手太重了……
t_en: Oops... I think we went a bit overboard...

t_ch: 发生了什么？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 想来是「缝合怪」最终还是没办法维系自身的形体吧。
t_en: I guess this "patchwork monster" couldn't maintain its form in the end.

t_ch: 这也太脆弱了吧？
t_en: That's WAY too fragile.

t_ch: 不，并不是我们的错。是这家伙自己的问题。
t_en: Well, it's not our fault. It's entirely her own fault.

t_ch: 我并不是幻造专家，只能粗浅解释一下——「幻造种」虽然是智慧生命，但和所有的「艺术」文脉相通。
t_en: I'm no imagenesis expert, so I can only offer a rather basic explanation. While the imagenae are intelligent beings, they resonate with all traditions of "art."

t_ch: 「艺术」这东西嘛，讲究一个主次有别、详略得当。如果一幅画、一本小说、一首歌，「全部都是重点」，那就等同于「完全没有重点」。
t_en: You see, when it comes to "art," it's all about knowing what to emphasize, balancing detail with brevity. If a painting, novel, or song tries to "emphasize everything," then it's essentially the same as "emphasizing nothing at all."

t_ch: 同理，一个幻造种如果全身都是「最受瞩目的部位」，那就会像这位「弗什么欧非手」一样，维持不住自己的形体，稍微挨几球棒，就变成现在的样子了。
t_en: Similarly, if an imagenae's entire body was made of the "most eye-catching parts," they'd end up like this "Frank-whatshername," unable to maintain their form. Just a few hits with a bat, and they'd turn into... well, this.

t_ch: 你知道的吧？这些尘灵是二相乐园的「最小愿力单元」…她也算是返璞归真了，真是个好结局，哈哈。
t_en: Did you know? These wispae are the smallest units of Wishpower in Planarcadia... She's really returned to her most basic form. What a perfect ending, haha.

t_ch: 但对我们就未必是好结局了！这家伙死掉了，这可没办法和朽叶小姐交差……
t_en: But that's definitely not a good ending for us! Now that she's dead, there's no way we can face Miss Kuchiba...

t_ch: 别担心，你们只是正当防卫，并没有做错任何事。
t_en: Don't worry, you were just acting in self-defense, you didn't do anything wrong.

t_ch: 太棒了，来得早不如来得巧，火花花顺利避开了最终决战！
t_en: Perfect! Talk about having impeccable timing! Sparxicle managed to dodge the final battle!

t_ch: 不会全身都变成尘灵了吧？
t_en: Don't tell me her whole body's turned into wispae?

t_ch: 桥依的眼睛还在吗？
t_en: Are Joye's eyes still there?

t_ch: 别担心，桥依小姐的眼睛没事。我刚才眼疾手快，在巴托莉小姐崩解之前，把它好好保管起来了，稍后我们一起去还给桥依小姐吧！
t_en: Don't worry, Miss Joye's eyes are fine. I was quick on my feet and managed to keep them safe before Miss Miranda disintegrated. Let's return them to Miss Joye together later!

t_ch: 这是我们在巴托莉•米兰达家中搜查到的日记…这个人的思想，实在是扭曲到了令人毛骨悚然的程度。
t_en: This is the diary we found during our search of Bartoli Miranda's house... This person's mind is twisted to a bone-chilling degree.

t_ch: 真是辛苦两位了。没能及时赶到现场提供支援，我也要向两位道歉。
t_en: Thank you both for your hard work. I also want to apologize for not being able to arrive at the scene in time to provide support.

t_ch: 没事啦，他们俩又没受什么伤～
t_en: It's alright, it's not like they got hurt or anything~

t_ch: 不要擅自替别人原谅啊！
t_en: Don't go around forgiving people on others' behalf!

t_ch: …但是当然了，我们两个也没受什么伤。没能活捉「千禧杀手」，我们应该向朽叶小姐道歉才对。
t_en: ...But of course, neither of us was seriously hurt. We should apologize to Miss Kuchiba for failing to capture the Millennium Killer alive.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，我也有做得……
t_en: Don't sell yourself short, I've also made some—

t_ch: 不是，你俩有完没完？别客套了，赶紧去把眼睛还给桥依宝宝吧！
t_en: Oh come on, you two! Cut the pleasantries already and go return those eyes to Baby Joye!

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}、不死途先生、火花小姐、朽叶小姐……
t_en: ...{F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, Mr. Ashveil, Miss Sparxie, Miss Kuchiba...

t_ch: 大家都在这里，桥依的眼睛也回来了。那就说明，你们顺利抓住「千禧杀手」了！
t_en: Everyone's here, and I've got my eyes back. That means you successfully caught the Millennium Killer!

t_ch: 呃…也不能算「抓住」吧！但反正她不会再有机会伤害任何人了～
t_en: Uh... I wouldn't say we "caught" her! But, in any case, she won't get another chance to harm anyone~

t_ch: 谢谢你们救了桥依！也谢谢你们，能为那些无辜被害的幻造种讨回公道。
t_en: Thank you for saving me! And thank you for bringing justice to those innocent imagenae victims.

t_ch: 即使凶手伏法，她造成的伤害也不会被磨灭。仍然有很多人失去了自己的家人和朋友，也仍然有很多人失去了自己的偶像。
t_en: Even if the culprit is brought to justice, the damage she caused cannot be erased. Many people have lost their families and friends, and many others have lost their idols.

t_ch: 这些伤口，恐怕还需要大家花上很长的时间去愈合。
t_en: These wounds will take quite some time for everyone to heal, I'm afraid.

t_ch: 总会有人带来希望，愈合伤口。
t_en: There will always be someone who brings hope and heals our wounds.

t_ch: 没错！所以偶像的工作就是带来希望！这项长期工作就交给我和桥依吧～
t_en: That's right! An idol's job is to bring hope! Leave this long-term mission to me and Joye~

t_ch: 很多伤口永远没办法愈合。
t_en: Some wounds never heal.

t_ch: 的确如此，所以「带着伤口活下去」，也总是人生的必修课。
t_en: Indeed, that's why "living with our wounds" is a lesson that everyone has to learn in life.

t_ch: 桥依小姐！你终于渡过难关了！我们就知道你能挺过去的！
t_en: Miss Joye! You finally made it! We knew you could pull through!

t_ch: 呜呜呜…桥依真的以为自己再也见不到你们了…好想你们啊……
t_en: *sniffles*... I really thought I'd never see you guys again... I missed you all so much...

t_ch: 我们也很想你，桥依小姐！呜呜呜呜呜……
t_en: We missed you too, Miss Joye! Waaaah... *sob*

t_ch: 也许我们永远没办法阻止善良的人们被邪恶侵扰，但至少今天，我们拯救了一份希望。
t_en: Maybe we can never stop evil from troubling kind people, but at least today, we saved a glimmer of hope.

t_ch: 哎呀～偶尔做些好事，心情竟然还不错～下次可以多尝试尝试！
t_en: Oh my~ Doing good deeds once in a while actually feels pretty nice~ Maybe I should try it more often!

t_ch: 感谢三位的协助。异常防御部也为三位提供了奖金，以表彰三位在「千禧杀手」一案中的卓越贡献。
t_en: Thank you all for your assistance. The Department of Aberration Defense has also provided you with bonuses in recognition of your outstanding contributions to the Millennium Killer case.

t_ch: 好耶！做好事还有钱拿！心情更好了！
t_en: Awesome! Getting paid for doing good deeds! I'm feeling even better now!

t_ch: …不过，不死途先生、火花小姐，虽然难以启齿，但眼下还有另一件事需要两位帮忙。
t_en: ...However, Mr. Ashveil, Miss Sparxie, although it's a bit awkward to say this here, there's another matter I need your help with.

t_ch: 请两位随我回到异常防御部，配合我们完成案件笔录。
t_en: Please come with me to the Department of Aberration Defense and assist us in completing the case report.

t_ch: 等一下，为什么只有我们两个？{NICKNAME}不用去吗？{F#她}{M#他}可是主要负责推理的那个人诶！
t_en: Hold up, why is it just the two of us? Doesn't {NICKNAME} have to be present? {F#She}{M#He}'s our main detective here!

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}是外宾。
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} is a foreign guest.

t_ch: …好吧，有道理。
t_en: ...Alright, fair enough.

t_ch: 等等，我也可以谈，我也可以是外宾。
t_en: Hold up, I can play that game too! I can also act like I'm a foreign guest.

t_ch: 你不可以是！乖乖和我一起去配合异常防御部的工作吧！
t_en: No you can't! Be a good sport and come help out with the Department of Aberration Defense's work!

t_ch: 哼，我反悔了！再也不要做好事了！
t_en: Hmph, I take it back! I'm never doing good deeds again!

t_ch: （回过头来想想才发现，我这可真是破了一桩大案啊！这样一来，我岂不是也能在二相乐园的犯罪史上留名了？嘿嘿。）
t_en: (Looking back now, I really did crack a major case! In that case, aren't I making a name for myself in Planarcadia's criminal history? Hehe.)

t_ch: （…感觉有点用词不当，把自己说得像罪犯一样了…总之是解决了一件大事，可喜可贺、可喜可贺。）
t_en: (...That came out wrong, I made myself sound like a criminal... Anyway, I took care of something major. How delightful! Love that for me.)

t_ch: 桥依与粉丝们正沉浸在重逢的感动中。还是先不要打扰他们了。
t_en: Joye and her fans are caught up in their emotional reunion. Better not disturb them for now.

t_ch: （什么情况，那座桥旁边怎么挤了这么多人？）
t_en: (What's going on? Why are there so many people crowded around that bridge?)

t_ch: 火花小姐好像已经到了，我们过去找她吧。
t_en: Looks like Miss Sparxie is already here. Let's go look for her.

t_ch: 小灰毛！桥依宝宝！我来啦～
t_en: Gray Hair! Baby Joye! I'm heeere~

t_ch: 走吧，该去找其他朋友聊聊了。
t_en: Let's go, time to chat with our other friends.

t_ch: 我还约了最后一位朋友，去见见对方吧。
t_en: I still have one last friend to meet up with. Let's go see them.

t_ch: 根据我的「主播直觉」，那边几个人最适合我们炒作！去找他们聊聊吧。
t_en: According to my "streamer's intuition," those folks over there would be perfect for our publicity stunt! Let's go chat with them.

t_ch: 快看，因巡就在那边。
t_en: Look, Izol is right over there.

t_ch: 我们该去问个清楚了。
t_en: We should go get some answers.

t_ch: 瞩目于我吧，这无与伦比的强大愿力！
t_en: Behold! My unparalleled, mighty Wishpower!

t_ch: 该死…怎么回事…感觉身体有些不受控制？
t_en: Blasted... What's going on... why does it feel like I'm losing control of my body?

t_ch: 不！等等…愿力…在拒斥我！
t_en: No! Wait... The Wishpower... It's rejecting me!

t_ch: 这怎么可能！我明明已经…踏入成神之境！
t_en: This can't be! I was so close to... ascending to godhood!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！你来看桥依啦～上次那件事，真是太感谢你了。如果没有你，桥依恐怕再也没机会做偶像了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you came to see me! Thank you so much for what you did last time. If it weren't for you, I probably would never have gotten the chance to be an idol again.

t_ch: 身体恢复得还好吗？
t_en: How's your recovery going?

t_ch: 放心好啦～美少女的恢复能力可是很强的！你看桥依水灵灵的大眼睛，哪里像是受过伤的样子？
t_en: Don't worry! Pretty girls like me have amazing recovery powers. See these big, sparkling eyes of mine? Do I look injured to you?

t_ch: 没留下什么精神创伤吧？
t_en: No lasting psychological damage, I hope?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，桥依的神经可是像筑桥的钢筋混凝土一样粗壮结实呢～才不会轻易就留下什么精神创伤！
t_en: Hehe, my nerves are as tough and solid as the steel and concrete used to build bridges. No way I'd get traumatized just like that!

t_ch: 你看，桥依不但生龙活虎，甚至还已经投身于下一场战斗啦！
t_en: See? Not only am I bursting with energy, I've already thrown myself into the next battle!

t_ch: …咳咳，重新自我介绍一下！
t_en: Ahem... Let me reintroduce myself!

t_ch: 我是二维市的过街天桥、也是奋斗中的少女偶像——桥依，叫我桥桥就好~
t_en: I'm the pedestrian overpass of Duomension City, and also an idol-in-the-making named Joye! But you can just call me JoJo.

t_ch: 目前，我正在参加二相乐园第三千二百五十八届「加油吧！寰宇美少女」超大型选秀节目！
t_en: Right now, I'm competing in Planarcadia's <unbreak>3,258</unbreak>th "Go for It! Miss Universe" mega talent show!

t_ch: 桥依目前票数<unbreak>29</unbreak>，现在只差一票，就可以挺进<unbreak>990</unbreak>强了！
t_en: I'm sitting at <unbreak>29</unbreak> votes now. Just one more is all I need to secure my spot among the Top <unbreak>990</unbreak>!

t_ch: 你还没投票的话，就帮帮我吧，好心的{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}！
t_en: If you haven't voted yet, help me out, kind {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 桥居然真能参加选秀？
t_en: So a bridge can actually enter the talent show?

t_ch: 我也是很努力才说服导演，成为历届选秀中唯一一座桥选手的！
t_en: I worked really hard to convince the director to let me be the show's first-ever bridge contestant!

t_ch: <unbreak>990</unbreak>强，有多强？
t_en: The Top <unbreak>990</unbreak>... How top are we talking?

t_ch: <unbreak>1000</unbreak>名里的正数<unbreak>990</unbreak>名！宇宙无敌强！
t_en: The <unbreak>990</unbreak>th out of <unbreak>1000</unbreak>. Top of the universe!

t_ch: 不过，强大的从来不是具体的数字，而是桥依的双腿明明无法自由跳动，但我却因为<unbreak>29</unbreak>份爱和期待，已经开始在成为偶像的道路上奔跑啦。
t_en: But what truly matters isn't the number itself. It's that even though I can't dance freely with my legs, those <unbreak>29</unbreak> shares of love and expectation have already set me running, straight down the road to becoming an idol.

t_ch: 我的票只留给最心动的偶像。
t_en: I'm only voting for the idol I love the most.

t_ch: 好啊，我一定会让你对闪闪发光的桥依心动。
t_en: You bet! I'll make sure you fall head over heels for the sparkling Joye.

t_ch: 我要证明，就算是一座桥，也可以成为千年一遇的美少女偶像！
t_en: I'm gonna prove that even a bridge can become a once-in-a-millennium idol sensation!

t_ch: 想聊聊你的创造者…
t_en: I'd like to talk about your creator...

t_ch: 问这个问题，是因为觉得桥依和二相乐园常见的幻造种不太一样吗？
t_en: You're asking this because you think Joye's different from the imagenae you usually see around Planarcadia, aren't you?

t_ch: 没有可以自由移动的艺术形象身体，保持着器物的工具属性，就连外表也是普普通通的天桥……
t_en: No tangible artistic form to freely move around in, still stuck with the utilitarian nature of an object, and even my appearance is just... an ordinary pedestrian overpass...

t_ch: 因为严格意义上来说，桥依不是一个在期待中被创造出的「幻造种」。
t_en: Because strictly speaking, Joye wasn't an imagenae brought into existence through someone's expectations.

t_ch: 我的创造者，只是一个平庸、以设计谋求糊口的桥梁工程师，他的设计撑不起纵横的高架，也无法联结寰宇的星系，他只创造出了我，为一笔小小的稿费。
t_en: My creator was just an ordinary, run-of-the-mill bridge engineer who designed structures to make ends meet. His designs couldn't support sprawling overpasses or link star systems across the cosmos. All he ever created was me, for a measly commission fee.

t_ch: 没有寄托、没有愿望、没有艺术、没有名字和眼睛，只是一座过街天桥。直到我自己给自己取了名字，自己找到自己的梦想——桥依就出现了！
t_en: No purpose, no wishes, no artistry, no name, no eyes. Just a pedestrian overpass. That is, until I gave myself a name and found my own dream... That's when Joye was born!

t_ch: 不过我很骄傲呢。我可是他设计生涯中唯一一座还没塌的小桥。
t_en: But hey, I'm pretty proud of that. I'm the only bridge in his entire design career that hasn't collapsed yet.

t_ch: …我现在想下桥了。
t_en: ...I wanna get off this bridge now.

t_ch: 欸嘿，晚了哦~而且你现在不是在桥下嘛！
t_en: Hehe, too late~! Besides, you're already under the bridge now!

t_ch: 你现在也不会塌的，对吧？
t_en: You're not gonna fall apart on me now, right?

t_ch: 欸嘿，我尽量~
t_en: Hehe, I'll do my best~

t_ch: 这事其他人知道吗？
t_en: Does anyone else know about this?

t_ch: 欸嘿，你猜？
t_en: Hehe, take a guess!

t_ch: 想聊聊你的梦想…
t_en: I'd like to talk about your dream...

t_ch: 在见面时向粉丝坦诚真实自我，本来就是偶像应该做的，请尽情向桥依提问吧~
t_en: Being real and letting fans see the real me. That's what being an idol is all about! So go on, ask me anything~

t_ch: 为什么想成为美少女偶像？
t_en: Why do you want to become an idol?

t_ch: 你或许可以想象，作为桥的我，日常生活就是在麻木茫然中目送，让无数过客从我身上走过。
t_en: You can probably imagine it. My daily life as a bridge is just standing here in numb bewilderment, watching countless strangers walk all over me.

t_ch: 可是LED大屏建起后，原本的过客却会为了那些屏幕中跃动的美好少女驻足，他们甚至站上天桥，兴奋地呼喊偶像的名字，为自推争辩不休。
t_en: But once those giant LED screens went up, the passersby who used to just walk on by started stopping in their tracks, drawn in by those radiant idols dancing across the displays. They'd even climb up onto the overpasses, shouting their faves' names in exhilaration, getting into heated debates over who's the best.

t_ch: 那一刻，激动、渴望涌上我的心头——「我也想拥有名字，我也想成为偶像！」就算诞生时没有被赋予愿望和期待也没有关系，桥依会有自己的愿望！
t_en: In that moment, excitement and longing welled up in my heart: "I wanna have a name too. I wanna become an idol!" It doesn't matter that I wasn't given wishes or expectations at birth. I will have my own dreams!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，桥依可是个很贪心的偶像！
t_en: Hehe, you can say I'm a super greedy idol!

t_ch: 如果真的实现了，那我一定会在「出道夜」高呼：「桥依想做，桥依去做，桥依成功做到！」
t_en: If I really make it happen, then on my debut night I'll shout at the top of my lungs: "Joye wanted it, Joye did it, Joye nailed it!"

t_ch: 哈哈，事实上我认真想过呢。如果真的可以被那么多人喜欢，走到成为偶像的那一天，我希望我的粉丝们能意识到：「偶像桥依也只是一座桥嘛！」
t_en: Haha, actually, I've given it some serious thought! If I really could get that many people to like me and make it as an idol one day, I hope my fans would realize: "Even as an idol, Joye is still just a bridge!"

t_ch: 我的意思是说，我不会离开这里。因为就算桥依真的成为了闪闪发光的美少女偶像，她作为一座天桥，让人们平安过街的最初使命也不会改变。
t_en: What I'm trying to say is, I'm not leaving this place. Even if I do become a dazzling superstar, my original mission as a pedestrian overpass — keeping people safe as they cross the street — will never change.

t_ch: 无论现在，还是未来。这才是偶像的意义嘛。
t_en: And that's exactly what being an idol is all about, whether it's now or in the future!

t_ch: 怎么就是千年一遇了？
t_en: How'd it become a once-in-a-millennium thing?

t_ch: 夸张的手法嘛~偶像是需要人设的，这样一说你不就记住桥依了吗？
t_en: It's called exaggeration~ Idols need a persona, y'know? With that, now you'll remember Joye, no?

t_ch: 再说，我也没有虚假宣传哦，这句话正确的扩写是「努力参加一千年选秀总能有一次遇到机会出道的美少女偶像」。
t_en: Besides, I didn't falsely advertise anything! A more accurate version of what I just said is: "Auditioning for a thousand years straight, I will eventually get my idol sensation at least once."

t_ch: 说实话，很少有偶像可以像我这样一千年不塌啦。
t_en: To be honest, very few idols can maintain their image for a thousand years without a scandal like me.

t_ch: 打算怎么做偶像？
t_en: So, what's your plan for making it as an idol?

t_ch: 尽管固化的四肢限制了我舞蹈的自由，但作为一座稳固的天桥，我可以比所有偶像都更加宠粉。
t_en: I might not be a great dancer with these stiff limbs, but as a rock-solid footbridge, I can spoil my fans way more than any other idol!

t_ch: 不需要买票，粉丝可以踩着我过桥，不需要付费，谁都可以跟我合照。免费的桥依招牌wink要不要？粉丝的公仆桥依静候您的驾到！
t_en: Fans can just walk all over me, no tickets required! It's totally free, and anyone can grab a selfie. Want a free signature wink? Joye, the people's footbridge, is at your service!

t_ch: 但是导演说我这样很没有商业价值。
t_en: But the director says I'm not "marketable" enough.

t_ch: 他希望有爆点，劝我「谈个地下恋、为人人都在践踏我卖个惨，至少上一次热搜，再写个『抱歉占用公共资源』的通稿……」
t_en: He wants a viral scandal. He told me, "Get a secret lover, play the victim since everyone's 'walking all over you' to get trending, then drop a canned apology for 'hogging public resources'..."

t_ch: 可是乐子神在上，天桥不就是公共资源吗！
t_en: But for The Laughter's sake, a footbridge literally is a public resource!

t_ch: 但你不早就是偶像了吗？
t_en: But... haven't you already made it as an idol?

t_ch: 其实，桥依目前还只能算不入流的小偶像啦！
t_en: Well, I'm still just a small-time, amateur one!

t_ch: 桥依偶尔在Livehouse里面进行一些小规模演出，积攒了一些数量不多，但都非常爱桥依的粉丝。现在这些粉丝也都很支持桥依的远大梦想。
t_en: I do small gigs at live houses sometimes, and I've got a small but super-loyal fan base. They're all totally behind my big dream now!

t_ch: 毕竟桥依想做到的可不止如此呢～桥依的终极梦想，是成为像火花小姐一样的超级偶像！
t_en: After all, I want to go way beyond that. My ultimate dream is to be a super idol, just like Sparxie!

t_ch: 火花小姐也说，她会全力支持我的梦想。她人真好！
t_en: Sparxie even told me she'd support my dream all the way. She's so wonderful!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，你也一定会支持我的吧？
t_en: {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, you'll definitely root for me too, right?

t_ch: 当然了！我一定支持！
t_en: Of course! You've got my vote!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，你真的特别特别特别好！桥依爱你哟～
t_en: Hehe, you're so, so, so sweet! Joye loves ya!

t_ch: 总觉得你的外形不太能做顶流。
t_en: Honestly, I'm not sure your "look" is cut out for the A-list.

t_ch: 没错，但这就是意义所在～
t_en: Exactly! But that's the whole point of it!

t_ch: 这世界上有很多人，都想要追逐自己「不配得到」的梦想吧？想做绘师的色盲、想做科学家的笨蛋、想做运动员的跛脚人，还有想做超级偶像的过街天桥。
t_en: Lots of people chase dreams that seem "out of their league," right? Like a colorblind artist, a scientist who's a bit slow, a limping athlete... or a footbridge that wants to be a super idol!

t_ch: 桥依想用自己的努力，传达一条信息——
t_en: I just want to work hard and send a message to everyone—

t_ch: 贪婪地前进吧！你配得到你想得到的一切！
t_en: Chase your dreams! You deserve everything you've ever wanted!

t_ch: 我还有事，下次再聊。
t_en: I've got stuff to do. Catch you later!

t_ch: 不要忘记为我投票，桥依一定会成为让你骄傲的超级偶像！
t_en: Don't forget to vote! Joye is going to be a super idol you can really be proud of!

t_ch: 还有事，下次再聊。
t_en: I've got stuff to do. Catch you later!

t_ch: 我再强调一次：名侦探「麦卡托」是不会输的，你的「薇克丝」根本就是在垂、死、挣、扎。
t_en: Let me emphasize this one more time: Detective Mercator never loses, and your Vexis is just grasping, at, straws.

t_ch: 胡说八道！我都怀疑你是不是真看过《迷局游戏》原漫画，不会是近期动画开播招来的新粉，随便看了两集就来这不懂装懂吧！
t_en: Nonsense! I doubt you've even read the original The Labyrinth Game comic. You're probably just some newbie who hopped on after the animation started airing, watched a couple episodes, and now you're here pretending you know everything!

t_ch: 你们在吵什么？
t_en: What are you guys arguing about?

t_ch: 我们在为了各自的偶像奋战，你离远点，小心误伤！
t_en: We're fighting for our own idols here, so back off! I don't want any collateral damage!

t_ch: 给我个面子，别吵了。
t_en: Do me a favor and knock it off.

t_ch: 您哪位？没看过我们漫画别瞎凑热闹。
t_en: And who are you supposed to be? Don't butt in if you haven't read our comics.

t_ch: 冷静一点，这好像是还没入坑的新人啊！怎么样，有兴趣来参加我们的「迷局游戏Only展」吗？感受侦探麦卡托和私刑制裁者薇克丝的智斗纠葛！
t_en: Take it easy. It looks like we've got a newcomer who hasn't fallen down the rabbit hole yet! So, are you interested in participating in The Labyrinth Game OnlyCon? Come experience the battle of wits between Detective Mercator and vigilante Vexis!

t_ch: 「薇克丝」？
t_en: Vexis?

t_ch: 我最爱的漫画《迷局游戏》的主角之一，极具魅力的反派角色！
t_en: One of the main characters from my favorite comic The Labyrinth Game, such a charming villain!

t_ch: 「麦卡托」？
t_en: Mercator?

t_ch: 伴随我童年的经典漫画《迷局游戏》的主角之一，神秘而正义的侦探。
t_en: A mysterious and righteous detective, one of the main characters from The Labyrinth Game, a classic comic from my childhood.

t_ch: 「迷局游戏」？
t_en: The Labyrinth Game?

s_ch: 看起来很能打的粉丝&冷静的粉丝
t_ch: 我们最爱的漫画！影响二相乐园的绝佳智斗艺术之作！
t_en: Our favorite comic! A masterpiece of tactical brilliance that influenced Planarcadia!

t_ch: 感兴趣的话千万要来看看，现在动画版正在热播中，只要你看了，一定会爱上薇克丝！
t_en: If you're interested, you absolutely have to check it out! The animation adaptation is airing right now, and I guarantee you'll fall in love with Vexis once you watch it!

t_ch: 还是爱上我们的智斗天花板麦卡托吧，她一定会胜利的！
t_en: Nah, fall for our queen of mind games, Mercator! She's destined to win!

t_ch: 一定会胜利？少吹牛啦，你可别忘了，麦卡托只是个外表跟小学生没什么两样的普通人，但薇克丝可是会咒杀的！只要知道一个人的容貌和名字……
t_en: Destined to win? Oh, please. Don't forget, Mercator is just some kid who look like a normal elementary school student, but Vexis can literally curse people to death! All she needs is someone's face and name...

t_ch: 普通人怎么了？杀人狂魔薇克丝根本不知道麦卡托的真名，这是她的制胜之策。
t_en: What's wrong with being normal? That psycho killer Vexis doesn't even know Mercator's real name. That's her winning hand.

t_ch: 我不允许你叫她杀人狂魔！请叫她「惩奸除恶•美艳动人•私刑制裁者」薇克丝，她是因为无法忍受城市的腐朽，才走上修罗之道的。她的牺牲，值得所有人的铭记！
t_en: I will NOT let you call her a psycho killer! Please address her properly as "Vexis, the Righteous Punisher of Evil, the Enchanting Beauty, and the Vigilante of Justice." She only walked the path of carnage because she couldn't stand the corruption of this city. Her sacrifice deserves to be remembered by all!

t_ch: …谁会记这么多前缀啊，我们麦卡托的前缀只有一个——「名侦探」！这才是人间正道！
t_en: ...Who's supposed to remember all those titles? Mercator only needs one: Detective! Now that is the true path of justice!

t_ch: 算了算了，跟你吵这些根本没有意义…如果当初椅子斯基没有突然病逝，能活着把结局画完就好了——心怀正义的她，一定会让名侦探麦卡托将薇克丝绳之以法！
t_en: Forget it, forget it, there's no point arguing with you... If only Chairsky hadn't passed away so suddenly and had lived long enough to finish the ending. Someone with her sense of justice would've definitely had Detective Mercator bring Vexis to justice!

t_ch: 胡说八道，椅子老师会画这么空洞乏味的结局吗？你根本就没看懂原作留下的伏线！薇克丝肯定会逍遥法外、笑到最后！区区身高一米二的小学生……
t_en: Nonsense! As if Master Chair would ever write such a hollow and boring ending. You clearly missed all the foreshadowing in the original! Vexis will definitely get away with it and have the last laugh! A mere first-grader who's only 4 feet tall...

t_ch: 你是在羞辱麦卡托的身高吗？她的智慧只是浓缩在了一具孩童般可爱的身躯里！
t_en: Is that an insult to Mercator's height? Her wisdom is just condensed into an adorably child-sized body!

t_ch: 这是羞辱？这是陈述事实！
t_en: An insult? No, just stating facts!

t_ch: 你上一次就是因为亵渎我们的偶像，才差点和我们麦卡托粉丝团团长「不死神探」当街互殴的，要不是有个会说话的猴子拉架，你今天说不准就得躺着来逛漫展了。
t_en: Last time you mouthed off and insulted our idol, you nearly got into a street brawl with the Ashen Detective, the head of our Mercator Fan Club. If that talking monkey hadn't stepped in to break it up, you'd probably be visiting this convention on a stretcher today.

t_ch: …又提这事，我懒得跟你吵了，你们麦卡托粉丝真扫兴！
t_en: ...Bringing this up again? I can't be bothered to argue with you. You Mercator fans are such killjoys!

t_ch: 哼，只是提醒你谨言慎行！
t_en: Hmph, just a friendly reminder to watch your mouth!

t_ch: （这俩人貌似不会打起来了，真没劲……）
t_en: (Looks like these two aren't going to start a fight... how boring...)

t_ch: （不过…「不死神探」和「会说话的猴子」…该不会是那个落魄侦探吧？他兴趣还挺广泛。）
t_en: (But... the Ashen Detective and the Talking Monkey... could it be that down-on-his-luck detective? He sure has a wide range of hobbies.)

t_ch: （一本没完结的漫画，竟然能有这么多粉丝为他吵架，真厉害啊。可惜我也没看过这个漫画，就不凑热闹了，先离开这里吧。）
t_en: (A comic that never even got a proper ending, and it still has fans arguing this hard... pretty impressive, honestly. Too bad I've never read it myself. I better stay out of this and get out of here.)

t_ch: 欸，这里是「迷局游戏Only展」，你怎么穿着《绒绒号》的Cos服就过来了…算了算了，咱们冷门作品爱好者还是要心胸宽广一些。
t_en: Hey, this is The Labyrinth Game OnlyCon, why'd you show up cosplaying The Fluffy... Ah well, we niche-fandom folks should be broad-minded anyway.

t_ch: 而且现在《迷局游戏》动画版正在热映，很快咱们也会变大热门的！
t_en: And with The Labyrinth Game animation blowing up right now, we'll become mainstream in no time!

t_ch: 你好，有什么同人志吗？
t_en: Hi, got any fan comics?

t_ch: 当然有。虽然「椅子斯基」死前没能画完结局让人遗憾，但这也给我们留下了无尽的二次创作空间呐！
t_en: Of course! While it's a shame that Chairsky passed away before finishing the ending, but it left us with infinite possibilities for fan creations!

t_ch: 你在我这里能看到的，都是封神级别的同人志，请放心试阅！
t_en: Everything you see here is god-tier, feel free to check them out!

t_ch: 《归案后，死对头为我挑囚服》
t_en: My Sworn Enemy Picked Out My Prison Uniform for Me After My Arrest

t_ch: 「追缉薇克丝的第五年，麦卡托将她缉拿归案了。没有争吵，没有歇斯底里，薇克丝接受了自己的命运。」
t_en: "In the fifth year of hunting down Vexis, Mercator finally brought her in. No shouting, no struggle, Vexis simply accepted her fate."

t_ch: 「但麦卡托终其一生也不敢停下追查罪恶的脚步。或许是因为，她始终忘不了薇克丝入狱前从她手中接过『M』码囚服时，那双忧郁的眼睛……」
t_en: "But for the rest of her life, Mercator never dared to stop chasing down evil. Perhaps because she could never forget those melancholy eyes Vexis had when she took the 'M' sized prison uniform from Mercator's hands... just before she was locked away..."

t_ch: 就买这个！
t_en: I'll take this one!

t_ch: 太有眼光了，多谢惠顾！
t_en: Great taste! Thanks for your business!

t_ch: 再看看其他的。
t_en: Let's check out the others.

t_ch: 《以我之名咒杀我》
t_en: Kill Me by My Name

t_ch: 「最终的对峙就在这样一个肃杀的秋夜结束了。『你是我最好的搭档，』薇克丝躺倒在地，悲伤地说：『也是我将死之时，唯一想要呼唤名字的人。』」
t_en: "Their final confrontation came to an end on a bleak autumn night. 'You were my best partner,' Vexis said as she collapsed to the ground, sorrow in her voice. 'And the only one whose name I wanted to call in my final moments.'"

t_ch: 「『是的，但是你少装了， 』麦卡托说，『现在被{RUBY_B#咒杀}呼唤{RUBY_E#}到要死的人是我。』」
t_en: "'Yeah, but drop the act,' Mercator said. 'Right now, the one who's being {RUBY_B#cursed}called upon{RUBY_E#} to death is me.'"

t_ch: 就买这个！
t_en: I'll take this one!

t_ch: 太有眼光了，多谢惠顾！
t_en: Great taste! Thanks for your business!

t_ch: 再看看其他的。
t_en: Let's check out the others.

t_ch: 《复活吧，椅子斯基！》
t_en: Rise Again, Chairsky!

t_ch: 「多年以后，面对空白的结局，所有《迷局游戏》粉将会回想起椅子斯基绘下薇克丝第一根发丝的那个遥远的下午。可故事不能就此结束——」
t_en: "Many years later, staring at the unfinished ending, all fans of The Labyrinth Game would remember that distant afternoon when Chairsky sketched Vexis's very first strand of hair. But the story cannot end here..."

t_ch: 「复活吧，椅子斯基！给我画完结局！」
t_en: "Rise again, Chairsky! Finish the ending for us!"

t_ch: 就买这个！
t_en: I'll take this one!

t_ch: 太有眼光了，多谢惠顾！
t_en: Great taste! Thanks for your business!

t_ch: 再看看其他的。
t_en: Let's check out the others.

t_ch: 我全都要。
t_en: I want them all.

t_ch: 你难以取舍，最终决定统统拿下。
t_en: Unable to choose, you decide to take them all.

t_ch: 乐子神在上！多谢惠顾！
t_en: By The Laughter above! Thanks for your business!

t_ch: 这里的同人志我全都要了。
t_en: I'll take all the fan comics here.

t_ch: 你难以取舍，最终决定统统拿下。
t_en: Unable to choose, you decide to take them all.

t_ch: 真是闪电般的购物，多谢惠顾！
t_en: Very decisive! Thanks for your business!

t_ch: 虽然你穿着别家Cos服来「迷局游戏Only展」的行为不值得鼓励…但不得不说，你Cos的「球棒浣熊」还真挺还原的，简直和本尊一模一样。
t_en: While I can't exactly encourage you wearing cosplay from other franchises to The Labyrinth Game OnlyCon... I gotta admit, your Baseball Raccoon cosplay is spot-on. You look just like the real thing.

t_ch: 说起来，今天展会上也来了个神还原「麦卡托」Coser，大家都在合影呐！我打算等会儿也过去看看。
t_en: Speaking of which, there's an insanely accurate Mercator cosplayer here today. Everyone's been lining up to take pictures. I'm planning to head over and check it out later.

t_ch: 你Cos得那么好，也该多学学人家，去自己该去的漫展！下次不许了啊！
t_en: Your cosplay is so good, you should learn from others and go to the conventions you're actually supposed to attend! Don't do this again next time!

t_ch: （真是懒得跟这家伙解释…话说雪衣应该会对「神还原的Coser」感兴趣吧？临走前去替她拍个照好了。）
t_en: (Can't be bothered explaining to this guy... Speaking of which, Xueyi would probably be interested in this so-called spot-on cosplayer, right? I should take some photos for her before I head out.)

t_ch: （感觉这里的同人志似乎都还挺有趣的，不妨再到处逛逛吧。）
t_en: (The fan comics here all seem pretty interesting. Maybe I should take another look around.)

t_ch: 这是一本「平行世界」设定的同人志。封面上的麦卡托和薇克丝都穿着仙舟风格的服装。
t_en: This is a fan comic set in a "parallel universe." On the cover, both Mercator and Vexis are wearing Xianzhou-style outfits.

t_ch: 何也？汝何不杀我？岂以赦我一命，示汝之宽？
t_en: Wherefore? Why dost thou not smite me? Dost thou spare my life merely to vaunt thy mercy?

t_ch: 如汝此等凶徒，本当斩决，不宜贷命。然若我手加于汝，吾与汝之恶，又有何异？
t_en: Thy sort doth merit but the steel, and not forgiveness. Yet should I slay thee with this very hand, wherein shall I be different from thy malice?

t_ch: 你实在看不懂这两个古风小生在说些什么…但雪衣说话好像也是这个味儿？她说不定会喜欢？
t_en: You honestly have no idea what these two classical-styled characters are saying... but isn't this kind of how Xueyi talks too? She might actually like this?

t_ch: …还是算了吧，二相乐园的作者们所理解「仙舟风格」总是有哪里怪怪的。
t_en: ...Never mind. The authors' interpretation of "Xianzhou style" in Planarcadia always feels a bit off.

t_ch: 这是一本印刷精美，画风精良的同人志。封面上的麦卡托和薇克丝都被画得特别可爱。
t_en: This is a beautifully printed fan comic with excellent artwork. On the cover, both Mercator and Vexis are drawn extra adorably.

t_ch: 薇克丝小姐，我可以捏捏你肉嘟嘟的小脸蛋吗？
t_en: Miss Vexis, may I pinch your chubby little cheeks?

t_ch: 不可以，麦卡托小姐。你是名侦探，而我是杀人鬼。我们这样的人，是不可以…啊！你不要突然捏我肉嘟嘟的小脸蛋呀！
t_en: You may not, Miss Mercator. You are a detective, and I'm a murderer. People like us shouldn't... Ah! Don't suddenly pinch my chubby little cheeks like that!

t_ch: 对不起，我实在是忍不住…你那肉嘟嘟的小脸蛋，实在是太可爱了！
t_en: Sorry, I just can't help myself... your chubby little cheeks are just too adorable!

t_ch: 虽然你没看过《迷局游戏》，但你十分确信这本同人志的作者也没有看过原作。
t_en: Even though you never read The Labyrinth Game, you are absolutely certain that whoever made this fan comic hasn't either.

t_ch: 看来每个同人圈都会被这种没看过原作的诡异二创侵蚀…如果这本同人志还卖得很好，那就更糟糕了。
t_en: Looks like every fandom gets plagued by these weird fan works from people who never touched the source material... And it'd be even worse if this fan comic sells well.

t_ch: 这是一本…呃，应该是同人志的东西。封面上写着《薇克丝与现代伦理学的超克》几个大字，全书约七百页，没有任何插图。
t_en: This is a... uh, seems to be some kind of fan comic. The cover reads Transcending Modern Ethics with Vexis. It is about seven hundred pages, without a single illustration.

t_ch: 「薇克丝之行为，可谓对现代伦理学语境中『他者』之彻底否定与再召唤。其以主权性之咒文介入实存秩序，乃试图在法之空场上重塑规范之超越性结构。」
t_en: "Vexis's actions represent a complete negation and re-evocation of 'the Other' in the context of modern ethics. Through sovereign charmwork interfering with the existential order, she attempts to reforge the transcendental structure of norms upon the void of the law."

t_ch: 「换言之，薇克丝并非单纯之杀人者，而是以暴力之形式对『合法性自身』发出质询。此一行动，恰是对义务论的戏仿性摧毁与价值重估的拟态性演绎。」
t_en: "In other words, Vexis is not merely a murderer, but rather one who questions 'legitimacy itself' through the form of violence. This action is precisely a parodic destruction of deontology and a mimetic interpretation of value re-evaluation."

t_ch: 「其自以为在致力于『实现正义』，实则坠入了『能指的能指』之无限递归，遂使『正义』沦为自我指涉的镜像游戏。」
t_en: "She believes she's striving for 'justice,' yet in truth she has fallen into an 'infinite recursion' of signifiers pointing to signifiers, reducing 'justice' to a mere self-referential game of mirrors."

t_ch: 你完全不知道这本同人志在写什么东西，只知道不管故事中的薇克丝杀了多少人，她都显然遗漏了一个。
t_en: You have no idea what this fan comic is about, but you do know that no matter how many people Vexis killed in the story, she very clearly missed one.

t_ch: 天哪，好可爱的小学生侦探，简直就是麦卡托本人！
t_en: Oh my gosh, what an adorable little grade school detective. It is like seeing Mercator in the flesh.

t_ch: 这真是角色扮演吗？怎么感觉连容貌都和麦卡托一模一样？
t_en: Is this really cosplay? How does she even look exactly like Mercator?

t_ch: 可你看她旁边那把长眼睛的椅子——
t_en: But look at that chair with eyes next to her...

s_ch: 兴奋的粉丝&笃定的粉丝
t_ch: 也和椅子斯基本人的漫画形象一模一样！
t_en: It looks exactly like Chairsky's comic self-portrait!

t_ch: 拜托了，请不要再围着我们……
t_en: Please, stop crowding around us...

t_ch: 什么情况？
t_en: What's going on?

t_ch: 你好，看镜头。
t_en: Hi there, look at the camera.

t_ch: 确实很可爱！
t_en: She's actually pretty cute!

t_ch: 等等……
t_en: Wait...

t_ch: 「球棒浣熊」？喂！你是「球棒浣熊」吧！
t_en: Baseball Raccoon? Hey! You're Baseball Raccoon, aren't you?

t_ch: 既然你也是漫画角色，应该明白我的处境，请帮我离开这里吧！
t_en: Since you're also a comic character, you should understand my situation. Please help me get out of here!

t_ch: 我只是来拍照的。
t_en: I'm just here to take pictures.

t_ch: 都可以都可以，总之先想办法让我离开！
t_en: Whatever, whatever, just find a way to get me out of here first!

t_ch: 纠正，是漫画取材自我的生活。
t_en: Correction, the comic is based on my life.

t_ch: 漫画也好，真人也好，总之拜托先想办法让我离开！
t_en: Comic, real person, whatever it is, please just find a way to get me out of here first!

t_ch: 小小人群而已，只要转移她们的注意力就能跑，你决定——
t_en: It's just a small crowd, if you can distract them, you can make a run for it. You decide to...

t_ch: 掏出球棒，暴力威胁。
t_en: Take out the bat and threaten with violence.

t_ch: 这…这是《绒绒号》的Coser？别动手，我让开我让开！
t_en: W—Wait... is that a cosplayer from The Fluffy? Don't hit me, I'm leaving, I'm leaving!

t_ch: 不好啦！《苍天航路绒绒号》的粉丝来砸场子啦！
t_en: Oh no! The fans of Fluffy Across the Blue are here to crash our party!

t_ch: 撒一把随便什么货币。
t_en: Toss around some random currency.

t_ch: 你从零钱包里掏出了一把各个世界的一般等价物，撒向人群。都是些散碎零钱，但效果如你预料。人们一拥而上，哄抢了起来。
t_en: You pull a handful of common currencies from different worlds out of your coin purse and scatter them into the crowd. It's just loose change, but the effect is exactly as you expected. People rush forward in a frenzy, scrambling to grab the money.

t_ch: 毕竟，天上掉的馅饼就算只值一毛，也没人能忍住不摧眉折腰。哎，热爱，面对现实总是一败涂地！
t_en: After all, even if the pie that falls from the sky is only worth a single penny, no one can resist bending down to grab it. Ah, passion always gets its butt kicked by reality!

t_ch: 干得漂亮，椅子小姐，趁现在——快跑！
t_en: Nice work, Miss Chair, now's our chance! Leg it!

t_ch: 人群一哄而散，你得以带着两位奇怪的新朋友逃离现场。
t_en: The crowd scatters instantly, giving you the chance to escape the scene with your two strange new friends.

t_ch: 到这里应该就算是躲开了…谢谢你。
t_en: We should be safe here now... Thank you.

t_ch: 为什么要跑？
t_en: Why are you running?

t_ch: 出cos被围住也正常吧……
t_en: I mean, getting swarmed while cosplaying is pretty normal...

t_ch: 唉哟，你不会真不明白吧？我不是Coser啊！就像你不是在Cosplay「球棒浣熊」那样！
t_en: Oh come on, you really don't get it? I'm not a cosplayer! Just like you're not cosplaying Baseball Raccoon!

t_ch: 我真不明白。
t_en: I really don't get it.

t_ch: 其实是「球棒浣熊」cos我。
t_en: Actually, Baseball Raccoon is cosplaying me.

t_ch: …我大概明白了，「球棒浣熊」是以你为原型的角色。唉，看来咱们的情况完全不同。
t_en: ...I think I get it now, Baseball Raccoon is a character based on you. *sigh* Looks like our situations are completely different.

t_ch: 但事到如今，我也实在有些走投无路…就和你实话实说吧。
t_en: But at this point, I'm really at my wit's end... so I might as well be straight with you.

t_ch: 我并不是在扮演「麦卡托」。我就是《迷局游戏》里的「麦卡托」本人。
t_en: I'm not pretending to be Mercator. I am THE Mercator from The Labyrinth Game.

t_ch: 至于我，则来自《迷局游戏》每卷单行本衬里上的「作者寄语」。椅子斯基老师为自己绘制的「自画像」，就是我「椅子小姐」啦！
t_en: As for me, I come from the "Notes From the Author" on the inside cover of each volume of The Labyrinth Game. The little "self-portrait" that Chairsky drew of herself is me, Miss Chair.

t_ch: 用更冰冷的话来讲——我们是未经异常防御部批准，擅自化为幻造种的「灾异」。
t_en: To put it more bluntly, we are aberrations, unauthorized imagenae who transformed without the Department of Aberration Defense's approval.

t_ch: 但这可不是我们主动做的哦！《迷局游戏》都断更十多年了！
t_en: But hey, this wasn't our doing! The Labyrinth Game hasn't been updated for over a decade!

t_ch: 我猜想，是因为「幻月游戏」期间，愿力充盈世间，才导致了我们未曾料想的诞生吧…另外也是因为《迷局游戏》最近动画化了，让这部作品再次成为了读者关注的焦点。
t_en: I suppose it's because during the Phantasmoon Games, when Wishpower fills the world, that led to our unexpected creation... Plus, The Labyrinth Game recently got an animation adaptation, which brought this work back into the spotlight among readers.

t_ch: 老作品翻红，我和椅子小姐却跟着成了异常防御部喊打喊抓的「灾异」，真令人恼火。
t_en: Our old work's gone viral, but now Miss Chair and I have become aberrations hunted down by the Department of Aberration Defense. What a pain in the neck.

t_ch: 别那么沮丧，瞧瞧，椅子斯基当年给我们画得有眼睛有腿儿的，多厉害啊。
t_en: Don't be so down! Look, Chairsky drew us with eyes and legs back then, how awesome is that?

t_ch: 如果她没死，这个故事一定会有完美的结局。粉丝们也是这样想着让我们诞生的吧。
t_en: If she hadn't died, this story would've had a perfect ending. That's probably what the fans were thinking when they created us, too.

t_ch: 可惜了，我这四条都是腿，没有手来学画画！
t_en: What a shame, all four of my limbs are legs, so I got no hands to learn how to draw!

t_ch: 但你跑得快啊！异常防御部追过来的时候，你扛起我就能跑。
t_en: But you can run so fast! When the Department of Aberration Defense was chasing us, you just scooped me up and ran!

t_ch: 还是自首吧，麦卡托。
t_en: Turn yourself in, Mercator.

t_ch: 有机会我也试试椅子。
t_en: I'll give that chair a try when I get the chance.

t_ch: 喂喂——
t_en: Hey, hey!

t_ch: 那我希望你是为了追凶，而不是跟我一样逃跑。
t_en: I hope you are here to hunt down the bad guys, not running away like me.

t_ch: 其实我也想过自首，毕竟我的人生准则就是「绝不破坏法律」…但是现在我还不能这样做，至于理由…我想你已经猜到了。没错，就像我和椅子小姐那样——
t_en: I did consider turning myself in, since my life principle has always been "never break the law"... but I can't do that right now, and as for the reason... I think you've already guessed it. That's right, just like with Miss Chair...

t_ch: 杀人不眨眼的私刑制裁者薇克丝，也来到了这个世界。
t_en: Vexis, the ruthless vigilante who kills without batting an eye, has also arrived in this world.

t_ch: 薇克丝，一个治安官世家出生的孩子，曾是我最好的搭档…却踏上了修罗之路。
t_en: Vexis, a child born into a family of security officers, was once my best partner... until she walked down the path of carnage.

t_ch: 只要见过容貌、念出姓名，对方就会如她预设的那样死亡。她依靠着这项超能力，从罪犯杀到同事，从罪大恶极的人杀到拦路的一切，最终…甚至将屠刀对准了无辜者……
t_en: Once she sees someone's face and speaks their name, they die exactly as she predestined. With this supernatural power, she killed her way from criminals to colleagues, from the heinously evil to anyone who stood in her path, until finally... she turned her blade on the innocent...

t_ch: 为了阻止她，在漫画的结局前夕，我以身入局，设下了一个陷阱：只要她试图咒杀我，就会立刻被捕，可惜…结局永远停在了那里，而犯罪者却逃来了此处。
t_en: To stop her, I stepped onto the board right before the comic's ending and set a trap. If she tried to curse me to death, she'd be caught immediately. But alas... the story froze at that moment, and the criminal escaped to this place.

t_ch: 作为最了解她的侦探「M」，我必须先阻止她继续犯下罪行，再去自首。
t_en: As Detective "M" who knows her best, I must first stop her from committing more crimes, then turn myself in.

t_ch: 你怎么证明她也在这里？
t_en: How can you prove she's here too?

t_ch: 因为我诞生了。正义从不独立存在，侦探只会为消灭罪恶诞生。
t_en: Because I was born. Justice never exists independently. Detectives are born only to eliminate evil.

t_ch: 那些杀人预告函也根本不是漫展物料，在其他地方，它们正被四处张贴。
t_en: Those murder calling cards aren't just convention merch. They're being posted everywhere else, too.

t_ch: 所以，听着，这不是危言耸听：薇克丝已经来到现实世界了，她将以那染血的唇和手，为这座欢愉的城市，带来暴烈而彻底的死亡。
t_en: So listen up, and I'm not being dramatic here: Vexis has entered reality, and with her blood-stained lips and hands, she will bring violent and absolute death to this city of Elation.

t_ch: 那还不上报治安官？
t_en: Why don't we report this to security officer?

t_ch: 小孩子做这些也太危险了！
t_en: It's way too dangerous for kids to do this!

t_ch: 我才不是小孩子，你这个失礼的浣熊！
t_en: I'm not a kid, you rude raccoon!

t_ch: 如果现在报案，因为缺乏证据，我一定会因为身份问题直接遭到拘捕，所以最好的方法，还是得先找到薇克丝本人。
t_en: If I report this now, I'll get arrested over my identity due to lack of evidence. The best course of action is to find Vexis first.

t_ch: 你有没有可靠的二相乐园侦探人脉？
t_en: Do you have any reliable detective contacts in Planarcadia?

t_ch: 有，那可太有了，还是我们的老朋友——「不死神探」。你满意地点点头，就找这位麦卡托粉丝团团长吧，他一定非常乐意效劳。
t_en: Oh yes, absolutely. And it is our old friend, the Ashen Detective. You nod with satisfaction. This head of the Mercator fan club will definitely be happy to help.

t_ch: 线人是侦探的命脉。你可以去一旁联系他，我不会探听过多。
t_en: Informants are the lifeblood of a detective. You can contact him over there, and I won't pry.

t_ch: 但愿你找来的帮手能提供些有用的线索。
t_en: Let's hope the help you find can provide some useful leads.

t_ch: 别紧张，正义侦探麦卡托的烟斗里，装不下失败。
t_en: Don't worry, failure has no place in Justice Detective Mercator's pipe.

t_ch: 抱歉，如果当初能给出一个结局……
t_en: Sorry, if only I could have given you an ending...

t_ch: 算了，椅子斯基已经做得很棒了。
t_en: Forget it. Chairsky already did a great job.

t_ch: 就在这里给不死途打个电话吧。
t_en: Let's give Ashveil a call right here.

t_ch: （拨通不死途的电话）
t_en: (Dial Ashveil's number)

t_ch: 「你能够确认吗？麦卡托？」
t_en: "Can you confirm that, Mercator?"

t_ch: 别看动画了，有委托人打电话找你。
t_en: Stop watching that animation, you've got a client calling.

t_ch: 您好，这里是「不死神探事务所」，我现在很忙，非常不高兴为您服务。
t_en: Hello, this is the Ashen Detective Agency. I'm busy right now and very unhappy to be of service.

t_ch: 「不要质疑我的答案…这些东西都在我的脑子里，快上！追寻犯罪的快感真让人血脉偾张！」……
t_en: "Don't question my answers... It's all in my head, come on! The thrill of chasing crime gets my blood pumping!"...

t_ch: 为麦卡托服务也不高兴吗？
t_en: Not happy serving Mercator either?

t_ch: 我管你麦…为麦卡托？咳咳，你也看《迷局游戏》？
t_en: I don't care about Mer... Mercator? *cough* You watch The Labyrinth Game too?

t_ch: 为我服务也不高兴吗？
t_en: Not happy serving me either?

t_ch: 为你服务…行吧，自从认识你，我就成了整个二相乐园最勤劳的侦探。
t_en: At your service... Well, ever since I met you, I've become the hardest-working detective in all of Planarcadia.

t_ch: 说吧，又惹什么事了？
t_en: Alright, what kind of trouble did you get into this time?

t_ch: 你向不死途讲述了刚刚发生的事情……
t_en: You told Ashveil about what just happened...

t_ch: 乐子神在上，「麦卡托」作为灾异出现在了二相乐园？她还说薇克丝也一起出现了？如果是别人跟我说这些，我肯定觉得对方在拿我寻开心…但既然是你说的……
t_en: By The Laughter, Mercator appeared as an aberration in Planarcadia? And she said Vexis was there too? If anyone else told me this, I'd think they were messing with me... but since it's coming from you...
